channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Vous pouvez perdre 10 000 euros si vous utilisez une carte Visa sur votre iPhone
item.description: Pouvoir voler 10 000 dollars sans contact depuis un iPhone verrouillé, c'est l'expérience assez dérangeante réalisée par la chaîne Veritasium, révélant une faille de sécurité qui date de 2021.

item.title: The He-Man Transformation in ‘Masters of the Universe’ Gave Us Chills
item.description: io9 saw 20 minutes of footage from the Mattel movie, which included the moment everyone was waiting for.

item.title: Ronan Farrow on Sam Altman’s ‘unconstrained’ relationship with the truth
item.description: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Ronan Farrow, one of the biggest stars of investigative reporting working today. He broke the Harvey Weinstein story, among many, many others. And just last week, he and co-author Andrew Marantz published an incredible deep-dive feature in The New Yorker about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, his trustworthiness, and the […]...

item.title: Character.AI’s new Books mode turns reading into roleplay
item.description: Mired in controversy and legal woes over concerns about its chatbots' interactions with users, particularly teens, Character.AI seems to be playing it safer with a new "Books" mode. The new format lets users step inside familiar worlds for a more structured roleplaying experience, one the company hopes will broaden perceptions of what AI roleplay can […]...

item.title: The Cybertruck of e-bikes is here to replace your car
item.description: It was at about 36 miles per hour that I decided the Infinite Machine Olto is not a bike. Sure, it has pedals, you don't need a license to ride it in most (but not all!) places in the US, and the folks at Infinite Machine assured me it is allowed in the bike lane. […]

item.title: Who goes there? Your Ring doorbell can now recognise up to 50 familiar faces, and let you know if a caller is a friend or a stranger
item.description: The new feature is rolling out now for premium subscribers, but you'll have to activate it manually if you want to try it.

item.title: Le Samsung Galaxy A27 perd (enfin) sa mythique goutte d’eau
item.description: Le célèbre leaker OnLeaks vient de lever le voile sur le design du prochain Galaxy A27. Principal changement à l'horizon : l'abandon définitif de l'encoche en goutte d'eau au profit d'un poinçon, alignant enfin ce modèle d'entrée de gamme sur ses grands frères les Galaxy A37 et A57.

item.title: Anthropic Plots Major London Expansion
item.description: As tensions with the US government mount, Anthropic has leased a new office with enough space to quadruple its 200-person headcount in London.

item.title: Meta raises Quest 3 and Quest 3S prices due to RAM shortage
item.description: Starting April 19, the price of the Meta Quest 3S (128GB) and Meta Quest 3S (256GB) will go up by $50 to $349.99 and $449.99, respectively. The price of the Meta Quest 3 is going up by $100 to $599.99.

item.title: 13 of the best Record Store Day 2026 releases, as picked by TechRadar — Pavement, Public Service Broadcasting and more
item.description: We're a music-loving bunch at TechRadar, just see our top Record Store Day 2026 picks (and why we love them)

item.title: 31 failles Chrome découvertes : Google lance une mise à jour d’urgence pour colmater des vulnérabilités critiques
item.description: Google déploie une mise à jour d'urgence sur Chrome. Celle-ci colmate plus de 30 failles, dont cinq vulnérabilités critiques.

item.title: 'Towards a full private digital workspace': Tuta debuts quantum-resistant cloud storage ahead of Google Drive, OneDrive
item.description: Tuta beat Google and Microsoft to quantum resistant cloud storage with zero knowledge architecture and invite only beta access starting April 16.

item.title: A computer without electricity? Mechanical computing concept 'could help improve people's lives' with all sorts of potential real-world uses
item.description: Mechanical computing platforms could operate where it isn't possible to use silicon chips.

item.title: What goes around comes around: Jury finds Live Nation guilty of working as an illegal monopoly to dominate the live music market, and I’m thrilled for the future of ticket prices at gigs
item.description: A US jury finds that Live Nation has been running an illegal monopoly to assume dominance over the live events market.

item.title: Crimson Desert is 'not a copy of other AAA games' according to The Witcher 3 director — and after my 175 hours of playtime and five million copies sold, it all makes sense
item.description: With high praise from a game director and five million copies sold, Crimson Desert isn't disappearing from gamers' discussions anytime soon — and it's deserved.

item.title: The New ‘Street Fighter’ Trailer Is Goofy as Hell
item.description: After a rocky first teaser late last year, 'Street Fighter' is back for round two.

item.title: Moft adds a tracker and shutter button to its magnetic tripod wallet
item.description: Moft's MagSafe wallets have long been overachievers with folding designs that double as smartphone stands. Its latest creation, the Moft Trackable Tripod Wallet, does even more. It carries forward the same functionality as Moft's original magnetic wallet that unfolds into a miniature tripod, but adds built-in tracking capabilities through Apple's Find My network, and a […]...

item.title: Taxe sur la copie privée : les smartphones, tablettes et PC pourraient coûter plus cher
item.description: Les ayants droit ont présenté leur proposition de barèmes en 2026 pour la taxe sur la copie privée. Ils souhaitent de fortes augmentations sur tous les appareils.

item.title: Sales of the Lego Artemis skyrocket by 320% at Argos — here's where to buy the popular set and 11 other space-themed builds
item.description: Space exploration fever has sent sales of the £55 Lego Artemis model soar 320% over the last couple of weeks — here's where it's available to buy, as well as several other space-themed sets.

item.title: Netflix reveals first image and 2027 release window for Charlie Vs. the Chocolate Factory — and it'll put a deliciously sour twist on Roald Dahl's classic tale
item.description: Charlie Vs. the Chocolate Factory will taste sweet to some fans, but sour to others.

item.title: Samsung casse le prix de son TV OLED le plus abordable : il chute à seulement 699 €
item.description: Bonne nouvelle pour celles et ceux qui veulent passer à l’OLED sans se ruiner. Samsung fait fortement baisser le prix de son téléviseur OLED le plus accessible, le TQ55S85F, qui tombe aujourd’hui à seulement 699 euros.

item.title: Tesla pris de vitesse ? Chery vend déjà son robot humanoïde en ligne
item.description: Connu pour ses ambitions sur le marché des véhicules électriques, le constructeur chinois Chery prend tout le monde de court en commercialisant son premier robot humanoïde, le Mornine M1. Bardé de capteurs hérités de la conduite autonome, ce concentré de technologie disponible en ligne s'affiche à près de 39 000 euros. Mais derrière cette fiche technique impressionnante se cache un point faible de taille. ...

item.title: Two years in the making: Canva AI 2.0 required an entire re-architecturing effort, and its yours to try if you can find the easter egg
item.description: The latest update takes Canva from a design platform with AI tools, to an AI platform that can design.

item.title: 'AI-crawled sites generate 320% more human traffic': An interview with Duda's Oded Ouaknine on the future of AEO
item.description: I spoke to Oded Ouaknine, CRO at Duda, about how AI is impacting website traffic and what small businesses can do about it.

item.title: Autonomous agentic assistants are at an 'absolute early adopter stage': Scheduling in Canva AI 2.0 is about to "democratize" that
item.description: Canva AI 2.0 includes an important new Scheduling feature that handles and repeats tasks for you in the background.

item.title: Canva’s AI 2.0 update goes all in on prompt-powered design tools
item.description: Canva has overhauled its design and workspace suite as it attempts to become the ultimate centralized hub for AI-powered content creation. The platform announced its Canva AI 2.0 update today, introducing updated tools and new prompt-based editing capabilities that allow users to make or adjust their work by describing what they want to create to […]...

item.title: Avant d’arriver en France, la pub sur ChatGPT change pour copier Google Ads
item.description: Deux mois après le lancement, déjà un changement de cap. La publicité dans ChatGPT va passer au clic, et les barrières d'entrée fondent à vue d'œil.

item.title: Behold the Punny Titles of ‘Rick and Morty’ Season 9
item.description: Plus, get your first look at Ridley Scott's new post-apocalyptic movie.

item.title: Congress Turns Up Pressure on DHS Over Palantir’s Role in Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Democrats are demanding answers about Palantir and other surveillance firms powering Trump’s hard-line immigration enforcement agenda.

item.title: Meta blames RAM shortage for $100 Quest 3 price hike
item.description: Meta is the next tech company to hike up hardware prices due to the global memory shortage. Beginning April 19th, Meta's 128GB Quest 3S VR headset will cost $349.99, the 256GB Quest 3S will cost $449.99, and the Quest 3 will cost $599.99. Those are increases of $50 for both Quest 3S models and $100 […]

item.title: Intel’s cheaper Panther Lake chips are for budget-friendly laptops
item.description: Intel is announcing a family of cheaper Panther Lake laptop processors called the Core Series 3 line. They're built on the same Intel 18A process as the higher-end Core Ultra Series 3 family of chips, but they have less of just about everything spec-wise. The Core Series 3 (non-Ultra) line encompasses six different chips, with […]

item.title: Canva’s AI assistant can now call various tools to make designs for you
item.description: The latest version of Canva's AI assistant lets users create editable designs with text prompts.

item.title: Plot twist! Canva just made another premium tool completely free for creators — I didn't expect this one, but I'll take it
item.description: Canva strips out from its newly acquired professional motion platform, and gives Affinity some big love, too.

item.title: De'Longhi's super-smart new coffee maker 'learns' your favorite hot and cold drinks, and brews them in moments
item.description: The De'Longhi Primadonna Aromatic has a huge menu of drinks, and pushes your favorites to the top of the list depending on the time of day.

item.title: Whoop is moving beyond wearables to test your blood, integrating the results with its AI-powered app — and its latest evolution is smarter than ever
item.description: Specialized Panels is a blood test which allows Whoop's in-app AI to 'deliver even more personalized, relevant insights' about your body.

item.title: Spotify debuts a new UI just for tablets
item.description: Spotify has a new look today for listeners on tablets. The streaming service’s updated tablet UI now provides adaptive orientation, switching between portrait and landscape layouts rather than simply resizing the interface when changing how the device is held.Spotify's tablet app now sports a collapsible sidebar so listeners can take advantage of their larger screen space when watching a music video or podcast. Parallel browsing lets you continue to scroll through the app while a video or lyrics are in the sidebar, and the "switch to video" toggle has been made more prominent. The new design h...

item.title: Canva starts previewing a more powerful version of its AI assistant
item.description: Adobe isn't the only company releasing a new AI assistant this week. Ahead of its Create event in Los Angeles today, Canva announced Canva AI 2.0. Building on its existing AI assistant, the company is billing the release as its most significant update since the platform first launched in 2013, and the culmination of years of investment to build its own foundational design models. As you might imagine, it all starts with a conversational interface that allows you to describe an idea or goal and the system will start generating a design to match. Under the hood, there's a new orchestration laye...

item.title: Allbirds stock is already falling after the AI pivot. History suggests investors should proceed with caution
item.description: When a company goes from footwear to AI cloud solutions, eyebrows are bound to rise, but pivots are much easier to announce than to execute. After rising by more than 580% in a single trading session yesterday, shares of Allbirds Inc. (Nasdaq: BIRD) fell this morning in premarket trading, at one point more than 30%.

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream director says development started in 2017 after discussing a 'special attachment' to the series with the producer, but had already 'squeezed all we could' out of the 3DS title
item.description: The creators of Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream have revealed that development began for the Nintendo Switch back in 2017, expressing an attachment to the series.

item.title: The Sony WH-CH520 Headphones offer 'simply superb value' at just $38 on Amazon
item.description: A low price for some of the best cheap headphones we've tested

item.title: « Pirater la vérification d’âge en moins de 2 minutes » : la nouvelle application phare de l’Union européenne déjà mise à mal
item.description: Aussitôt sortie, aussitôt piratée. L'application de l'Union européenne permettant la vérification d'âge serait criblée de problèmes.

item.title: Fashion retailer Express left customers’ personal data and order details exposed to the internet
item.description: Retail giant Express was publicly spilling customer information to the open web. The bug is now fixed after TechCrunch alerted Express, but the company would not say if it plans to notify customers.

item.title: Amazfit dévoile la Cheetah 2 Pro, une montre en titane avec lampe torche, à moins de 450 €
item.description: Amazfit lance la Cheetah 2 Pro, une montre conçue pour les sportifs avec un boîtier en titane, une lampe torche intégrée et un suivi avancé des performances sportives.

item.title: Un télescope virtuel de la taille de la Terre a mesuré des jets d’un trou noir à 10 000 soleils de puissance !
item.description: Une équipe de chercheurs des universités Curtin et d'Oxford a franchi une étape majeure dans l'étude des trous noirs en révélant la puissance et la dynamique des jets de matière émanant de Cygnus X-1, le premier trou noir confirmé. Ces découvertes ouvrent de nouvelles perspectives sur...

item.title: The 10 Best MagSafe Phone Grips for Your Butter Fingers (2026)
item.description: Keep your phone firmly in hand and add some personality with these comfortable, durable, and nifty smartphone grips.

item.title: This simulation startup wants to be the Cursor for physical AI
item.description: Antioch raised a $8.5 million seed round to create simulation tools for a new generation of robot builders.

item.title: Turtle Beach reveals the successor to the Stealth Pro, one of our favorite premium gaming headsets, featuring CrossPlay 2.0 multiplatform switching and Dolby Atmos
item.description: Turtle Beach has announced the Stealth Pro II wireless multiplatform gaming headset, the successor to the acclaimed Stealth Pro.

item.title: Avec la flambée des prix, cette RTX 5070 en promo devient une vraie bonne affaire pour les gamers
item.description: Avec la crise des composants et la flambée des prix sur les cartes graphiques, cette offre tombe plutôt bien. La MSI RTX 5070 se positionne comme un modèle taillé pour le gaming en 1440p ultra. Bonne nouvelle : elle est actuellement proposée à 599,99 euros au lieu de 689 euros, ce qui en fait une belle opportunité à saisir.

item.title: Claude va vérifier votre identité… à l’aide du prestataire à l’origine de la fuite Discord
item.description: Anthropic vient d'ajouter votre passeport à la liste des conditions d'utilisation de Claude. Ce n'est pas Washington qui l'exige.
